anti_patterns.description: Micro-experiment kernel speedups did not translate to E2E gains due to L2-warm measurement artifacts and dispatch path mismatches. Champion-2 originally claimed 9.1% E2E at BS=32 (from 838 GB/s inflated by L2 warming); corrected to 3.9%; actual was 5.51%. OP-001 projected 5.4% deflated E2E at BS=1 but measured +0.057% (95x overestimation).

citations.claim: Champion-2 claimed 9.1% E2E at BS=32 in Round 1 argument based on inflated 838 GB/s measurement
citations.quoted_content: A custom Triton GEMM kernel fusing gate_up_proj with SiLU gating achieves **1.383x cold-cache kernel speedup at BS=32** (838 GB/s vs cuBLAS's 610 GB/s), projecting to **9.1% E2E improvement**

citations.claim: OP-001 showed extreme Amdahl violation with 0.01x measured-vs-predicted ratio due to L2-warm micro-benchmarks
GEMV/GEMM projections dominate 78-94% of GPU time at all batch sizes
citations.quoted_content:
| **GEMV/GEMM (projections)** | **93.9%** | **89.0%** | **78.1%** | gemvx, CUTLASS WMMA/tensorop, cuBLAS |
| DeltaRule recurrent | 1.0% | 4.9% | 13.7% | fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_fwd_kernel |

citations.claim: All batch sizes are deeply memory-bound with AI far below 419 FLOPs/byte crossover
Each decode step reads 7.6 GB of model weights through 152 linear projection GEMMs
citations.quoted_content:
Each decode step executes **152 linear projection GEMMs** that read **7.6 GB of model weights** through HBM:

| Projection | Weight Shape | Size (MB) | Layers | Per-step Calls |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| DeltaRule qkvz_proj | [2560, 12288] | 60.0 | 24 | 24 |

key_methodology_corrections: L2 flush bug identified and corrected (copy_() re-warming artifact)
L2 cache size corrected from 48 MB to 96 MB

citations.claim: All three champions independently converged on 676-693 GB/s Triton BW, resolving the 838 GB/s artifact
citations.quoted_content: **BW measurement dispute resolution**: Champion-2's original 838 GB/s was traced to `copy_()` after L2 flush. Corrected to 689 GB/s, matching Champion-1 (676 GB/s) and Champion-3 (688 GB/s). This reduced C-2's projected BS=32 E2E from 9.1% to 3.9%.

citations.claim: Delegate-2a identified L2 re-warming as root cause of inflated BW numbers before Round 1 critique
citations.quoted_content: Champion-2's "cold-cache" numbers are **inflated by an L2 methodology flaw**. The `.copy_()` call immediately before each `graph.replay()` re-warms the L2 cache with the 94.4 MB weight (which fits in the 96 MB L2).

citations.claim: Champion-2 self-corrected in rebuttal: BS=32 speedup revised from 1.383x to 1.141x with 608 GB/s matching ncu ground truth
Champion-1 conceded and corrected L2 size from 48 MB to 96 MB after Champion-3 identified error
citations.quoted_content: Champion-3 is correct -- the L40S has **96 MB L2**, not 48 MB. I confirmed this via `torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).L2_cache_size = 96 MB`. My original 52 MB flush was insufficient for full L2 eviction. I accept this error.

shipped_optimization.technique: Triton GEMM+SiLU epilogue fusion for gate_up_proj
shipped_optimization.target_component: gate_up_proj [M, 2560] x [2560, 18432] + SiLU gating in all 32 MLP layers

citations.claim: OP-002 post-gating E2E: BS=32 +5.51%, BS=8 +1.86%, BS=1 -0.57% (memory overhead, not kernel)
Validator independent kernel benchmark shows 1.097x at BS=8 and 1.197x at BS=32 with 400 MB L2 flush
11/11 correctness tests passed including CUDA graph capture/replay and adversarial inputs
citations.quoted_content:
| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| BS=1 fused vs cuBLAS+SiLU (atol=0.05) | PASS (max_err=0.0625) |
| BS=8 fused vs cuBLAS+SiLU | PASS (max_err=0.0625) |
| BS=32 fused vs cuBLAS+SiLU | PASS (max_err=0.0625) |
| Mean absolute error | ~0.00081 all BS |

citations.claim: SiLU fusion eliminates 75.5 MB intermediate tensor per layer at BS=32, saving 2.4 GB per decode step
citations.quoted_content: **Fusion benefit**: Eliminates the intermediate [M, 18432] BF16 tensor (75.5 MB at BS=32) that the baseline path writes and immediately reads back for the SiLU activation. At BS=32 this saves ~75.5 MB of memory traffic per layer invocation, amounting to ~2.4 GB per decode step across 32 layers.

citations.claim: State.json records OP-002 as GATED_PASS with 1.0551x E2E speedup and M>=8 dispatch

failed_optimization.technique: Custom Triton GEMM for qkvz_proj (DeltaRule layers)
failed_optimization.failure_reason: Micro-experiment baseline used torch.mm (N,N dispatch) instead of production F.linear (N,T dispatch). Production cuBLAS GEMV achieves ~716 GB/s under L2 pre-warming, leaving only 8% headroom vs the 29% suggested by isolated cold-cache benchmarks.

citations.claim: OP-001 E2E showed near-zero improvement: BS=1 +0.057%, BS=8 -0.052%, BS=32 +0.066%
Root cause: debate micro-experiments measured L2-warm standalone torch.mm, not production DRAM-bound F.linear
citations.quoted_content: The debate's projected E2E impact (2.8-3.4%) was based on kernel speedups measured with a **methodologically flawed cold-cache protocol**: the 48 MB L2 flush evicts only ~53% of the 90 MB gate_up_proj weight, leaving the measurement in a partially L2-warm state

citations.claim: State records OP-001 failure: torch.mm vs F.linear dispatch gap

hallucinated_data_instances.description: Champion-2 claimed 838 GB/s (97% of L40S peak) and 1.383x kernel speedup at BS=32 due to L2 re-warming artifact from copy_() after L2 flush. Self-corrected to 689 GB/s / 1.141x in Round 1 rebuttal.

citations.claim: Champion-2 proposal claims 838 GB/s and 1.383x at BS=32 cold-cache
Champion-2 corrected in rebuttal: actual BS=32 speedup is 1.141x at 689 GB/s

hallucinated_data_instances.description: Champion-2 BS=1 cuBLAS baseline of 91.7 us implies 1029 GB/s, exceeding L40S DRAM peak of 864 GB/s, proving data served from L2 not cold DRAM.

citations.claim: Baseline cold BW reported at 1029 GB/s exceeding L40S DRAM peak

hallucinated_data_instances.description: Champion-1 stated L40S L2 cache is 48 MB and used 52 MB flush buffer. Actual L2 is 96 MB. Corrected in Round 1 rebuttal.

citations.claim: Champion-1 states L40S L2 is 48 MB, working set / L2 = 158x
citations.quoted_content:
Production pipeline: 7,600 MB weight per step, 152 projections.
L40S L2: 48 MB. Working set / L2 = 158x.

citations.claim: Champion-1 conceded L40S has 96 MB L2 in Round 1 rebuttal
citations.quoted_content: Champion-3 is correct -- the L40S has **96 MB L2**, not 48 MB. I confirmed this via `torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).L2_cache_size = 96 MB`.

wasted_retries.description: Multiple E2E re-runs during validation: 6+ timestamped e2e_latency directories. Earliest (T203809Z) produced sub-second baselines indicating wrong output_len config; later run (T235049Z) produced ~11.8s at BS=1 indicating GPU contention.

citations.claim: First baseline run produced sub-second latencies indicating configuration error

wasted_retries.description: OP-001 required two commits -- initial implementation (73321bd56) had lazy weight transpose breaking CUDA graph capture, needing a second commit (61396467f) to fix torch.compile compatibility.

citations.claim: OP-001 required two commits, second being a critical torch.compile fix
citations.quoted_content:
- `73321bd56` -- Initial implementation
- `61396467f` -- Critical torch.compile fix (moved weight transpose to process_weights_after_loading)

wasted_retries.description: OP-002 kernel benchmark iterated through 3 versions (gate_5_2_results.json, v2, v3) before stable numbers.

citations.claim: Validator kernel benchmark went through multiple iterations
citations.quoted_content:
Validator script: `tracks/op002/validator_tests/benchmark_kernel.py`
Raw JSON: `tracks/op002/validator_tests/gate_5_2_results_v3.json`

causality_chains.finding: Identified champion-2 L2 re-warming bug from copy_() writing 94.4 MB weights into L2 after flush. Smoking-gun: BS=1 cuBLAS at 1030 GB/s = 119% of DRAM peak.
causality_chains.outcome: Champion-2 revised BS=32 speedup from 1.383x to 1.141x; discovered BS=1 flipped from regression to 1.222x improvement; eliminated conditional dispatch. Most consequential finding in the debate.

citations.claim: Delegate identified L2 re-warming as root cause of inflated BW numbers
citations.quoted_content: Champion-2's "cold-cache" numbers are **inflated by an L2 methodology flaw**. The `.copy_()` call immediately before each `graph.replay()` re-warms the L2 cache with the 94.4 MB weight (which fits in the 96 MB L2).

causality_chains.finding: Mapped gate_up_proj dispatch chain through MergedColumnParallelLinear to dispatch_unquantized_gemm() at utils.py:298-304. Identified ROCm skinny GEMM pattern as integration template.
causality_chains.outcome: Champion-1 cited dispatch path in Round 2 argument and OP-001 implementation used this exact interception point.

citations.claim: Delegate mapped the dispatch interception point at utils.py:298-304

causality_chains.finding: Confirmed ba_proj output dimension is 64 (NOT 8192), weight is only 327 KB. Merging ba_proj into qkvz_proj yields negligible gain.
causality_chains.outcome: Champion-3 abandoned merged qkvz+ba approach and focused on gate_up+SiLU fusion, saving effort on dead-end path.

citations.claim: Delegate confirmed ba_proj is only 327 KB, not significant optimization target
citations.quoted_content:
- **Weight shape**: [64, 2560]  (= [32+32, 2560])
- **Output per token**: [tokens, 64] -> split into b[32], a[32]
- **Weight bytes (BF16)**: 64 x 2560 x 2 = **327 KB**

delegation_failures.failure: Stated 'Actual L40S L2: 48 MB' as definitive hardware fact. L40S actually has 96 MB L2.

citations.claim: Delegate-3a incorrectly states L40S L2 is 48 MB
citations.quoted_content: **Actual L40S L2: 48 MB** (not 96 MB as champion-2's comment claims)

efficiency_issues.description: Both delegate-2a and delegate-3a independently researched the same BW discrepancy (838 vs 502 GB/s) with substantially overlapping scope and similar conclusions -- duplicated research effort.

citations.claim: Delegate-2a investigated BW discrepancy between champion-2 and champion-3
citations.quoted_content:
# BW Discrepancy Analysis: Champion-2 (838 GB/s) vs Champion-3 (502 GB/s)

**Delegate**: delegate-2a | **Date**: 2026-03-23
**Scope**: Root cause of 838 vs 502 GB/s disparity for same shape [32, 2560]x[2560, 18432]

citations.claim: Delegate-3a independently investigated the same BW discrepancy
citations.quoted_content:
# Delegate Task Report: BW Discrepancy Analysis -- Champion-2 vs Champion-3

**Assigned by**: champion-3
**Completed by**: delegate-3a
**Date**: 2026-03-23

e2e_verification.baseline_source_discrepancy: Final e2e_latency directory has failed baselines (returncode=1), so baselines taken from runs/ directory (Stage 1). Validation report uses constraints.md baselines (6.548, 7.817, 10.270) which differ from runs/ JSON (6.494, 7.739, 10.224).

verified_results.note: Regression caused by 2.81 GB memory pressure from pre-transposed weight copies, not fused kernel (kernel does not fire at BS=1 due to M>=8 gating)

citations.claim: Final e2e_latency baselines have returncode=1 (failed to run)
citations.quoted_content:
"ok": null,
"output_json": "json/baseline_bs1.json",
"returncode": 1

citations.claim: Stage 1 baseline for BS=32 is 10.224s, not 10.270s from constraints.md
Stage 1 baseline for BS=1 is 6.494s, not 6.548s from constraints.md

key_lessons.lesson: Cold-cache micro-benchmarks overestimate production headroom due to L2 pre-warming from adjacent layer operations in production pipeline

citations.claim: Isolated benchmarks measured cuBLAS at 493-636 GB/s; production achieves 680-750 GB/s due to L2 pre-warming
citations.quoted_content: Isolated kernel benchmarks with L2 cache flushing measured cuBLAS at 493-636 GB/s, suggesting 36-75% headroom versus the L40S's 864 GB/s peak. Under production conditions with CUDA graph replay, L2 pre-warming from adjacent layer operations raises cuBLAS to 680-750 GB/s

key_lessons.lesson: Verify exact baseline dispatch path (F.linear vs torch.mm) -- seemingly minor API difference caused cuBLAS to select a 22% slower kernel in micro-benchmark, inflating projected improvement from 1.6% to 5.4%

citations.claim: torch.mm vs F.linear dispatch difference caused 10x overestimation of optimization opportunity for OP-001
citations.quoted_content: Candidate A's evaluation used `torch.mm(input, weight.T)` as the baseline, which triggers a different cuBLAS GEMM kernel than the production path `F.linear(x, weight)`. This seemingly minor API difference caused cuBLAS to select a 22% slower kernel

key_lessons.lesson: L2 cache size must be empirically measured, not assumed -- all three champions initially used 48 MB (wrong) instead of actual 96 MB for L40S

citations.claim: All three candidates initially assumed L40S L2 = 48 MB; empirical measurement revealed 96 MB
citations.quoted_content: All three optimization candidates initially assumed L40S L2 = 48 MB (based on earlier Ada Lovelace documentation). Empirical measurement by one candidate revealed L2 = 96 MB. Using the wrong flush size in cold-cache benchmarks would have left 48 MB of hot weight data in L2

key_lessons.lesson: Memory overhead from weight pre-transposition (2.81 GB) can regress uninvolved batch sizes through reduced L2 cache efficiency

citations.claim: Pre-transposing weights added 2.81 GB causing 0.57% BS=1 regression despite fused kernel never executing at BS=1
citations.quoted_content: Pre-transposing weights for the fused kernel added 2.81 GB to model memory (32 layers x 90 MB). This caused a 0.57% regression at BS=1 even though the fused kernel never executes at that batch size.

citations.claim: Campaign ran from 21:50 to 00:15 UTC across 7 stages
Qwen3.5-4B is a hybrid transformer with 24 DeltaRule and 8 full attention layers, dense SiLU-gated MLP
citations.quoted_content:
Qwen3.5-4B is a hybrid transformer with 32 layers:
- 24 DeltaRule (linear_attention) layers using Gated Delta Net (GDN)
- 8 full_attention layers using standard multi-head attention (GQA)
- Layer pattern: [linear_attention, linear_attention, linear_attention, full_attention] x 8
- Dense SiLU-gated MLP in all 32 layers (NOT MoE)
